marketState: neutral, front-month sitting almost exactly at fair value with conflicting fundamental and technical signals
summary: NG is trading right on top of every fair-value estimate ($2.95 front vs $2.92-$3.02 fair across now-to-plus21D, all flagged FAIR), so there is no mispricing edge to trade in either direction. Fundamentals pull both ways: 8-16 day weather demand is extreme (+35% vs 5y, above the top of the historical range) which argues for tighter near-term storage builds, but the storage report itself just printed a bearish surprise (+22 Bcf vs forecast, +180 Bcf surplus to 5y) and seasonality reliability is flagged Low this week (59pp spread) with only 38% historical odds of higher prices 3-4 weeks out. Managed money is net short at the 8th percentile of its 5-year range, an unusually crowded short that raises squeeze risk if the heat demand materializes. Technicals are split by timeframe: 5m/15m are bullish (RSI 62-65, MACD positive, 50>200 EMA) and 4h is stretched above its upper Bollinger band, while daily/weekly trend and momentum remain bearish (RSI 39-42, MACD negative, 50<200 EMA), so there is no clean directional consensus on any horizon right now.

longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk, scale in 2 tranches
longTerm.rationale: Extreme 8-16D cooling demand (+35% vs 5y, above the historical max) and an unusually crowded managed-money short (8th percentile of 5y range) both argue for upside risk if heat verifies and shorts are forced to cover, and price is not overvalued anywhere on the fair-value curve to fade. Conviction is capped at low because storage is running a +180 Bcf surplus with a bearish weekly surprise and seasonality reliability is flagged Low with only 38% historical odds of higher prices 3-4 weeks out.
longTerm.invalidation: Storage surplus widens further on above-forecast builds, or 8-16D heat forecast fades back toward normal (rangePos back inside 5y range) — either would remove the main bullish driver.

shortTerm.rationale: Intraday momentum (5m/15m bullish, EMA 50>200) conflicts with the daily/weekly downtrend (RSI 39-42, MACD negative, EMA 50<200), and 4h is already stretched above its upper Bollinger band, so chasing either direction here has poor risk/reward. Front price is also exactly at fair value with next-month spread pricing FAIR, giving no near-term mispricing to lean on.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a pullback into the $2.80-$2.85 fair-value/support zone to buy with the intraday trend, or a daily close back below $2.85 confirming the higher-timeframe downtrend to sell.

keyDrivers: 8-16D weather demand +35.1% vs 5y, above the top of the historical range — strong incoming cooling demand
Storage surplus +180 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +22 Bcf weekly surprise caps the fundamental upside
Managed money net short at the 8th percentile of its 5-year range — crowded short raises squeeze risk
Price sits almost exactly at fair value on every horizon (now through plus21D) — no mispricing edge
Technicals diverge by timeframe: 5m/15m bullish vs daily/weekly bearish trend and momentum
risks: Seasonality reliability flagged Low (59pp 10-90% spread) — seasonal pattern is not trustworthy this week
4h chart is stretched above its upper Bollinger band, raising near-term pullback risk
Extreme heat forecast could fade on the next weather update, quickly removing the main bullish catalyst and leaving the storage surplus as the dominant (bearish) driver
disclaimer: Informational only, not financial advice. Auto-generated hourly.
